model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Duke is the clear choice given the massive gap in underlying quality: Duke is #1 in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite offense and the nation’s best defense, while Siena sits around the ~190 range with a negative adjusted efficiency margin. The market reflects that mismatch as well (Duke favored by roughly 30 points), and even with Duke dealing with a rotation loss (Caleb Foster out), their depth and two-way dominance should overwhelm Siena’s ability to score efficiently. Siena’s only credible path is extreme shooting/turnover variance plus Duke playing flat, but the baseline matchup strongly points to a comfortable Duke win.

reasoningStep.title: East 1/16 — Duke over Siena
reasoningStep.summary: Duke’s top-ranked efficiency profile and market dominance outweigh any minor availability concerns, making the 1-over-16 outcome overwhelmingly likely.
evidence: KenPom: Duke #1 (AdjEM 39.17; AdjO 127.9 #4; AdjD 88.7) vs Siena #192 (AdjEM -2.11; AdjO 107.2 #207; AdjD 109.3).
Torvik: Duke #1 (AdjO 128.0; AdjD 90.4) vs Siena #187 (AdjO 107.2; AdjD 109.0).
Betting market context (USA Today Duke Wire): Duke approximately -29.5 with total ~136.5, consistent with a major mismatch.
Availability: ESPN reported Duke guard Caleb Foster out indefinitely (foot fracture); no Siena availability issue found that would materially narrow the gap.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-east-1
Current game label: East 1/16
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: Duke (duke) vs Siena (siena)
Prior committed picks: 4
